title: H.A.R.B.O.R
fullTitle: Hierarchical Analysis for Reference-Guided Biodiversity Organism Recognition
description: Open-set eDNA taxonomy inference system built for large-scale biodiversity recognition from environmental DNA sequences. Combines fast deterministic similarity checks, deep CNN-based sequence embeddings, and statistical novelty detection.
overview: HARBOR is an open-set eDNA taxonomy inference system built for large-scale biodiversity recognition from environmental DNA sequences. Traditional taxonomy systems force every sequence into an existing classification – even when the sequence belongs to an unknown organism. HARBOR solves this by combining fast deterministic similarity checks, deep CNN-based sequence embeddings, and statistical novelty detection to produce three honest output types: high-confidence known assignments, explicit NOVEL flags, and explainability metrics. It is designed to scale to multi-marker reference databases (~2.8GB) in alignment-free mode without sacrificing scientific defensibility.

features.label: Open-Set / Novelty-Aware Classification
features.description: Distinguishes known sequences from out-of-distribution ones. Novel sequences receive explicit NOVEL flags.

features.label: Multi-Stage Hybrid Inference
features.description: Three-stage pipeline: Deterministic similarity → Embedding-based ANN search → Statistical novelty detection.

features.label: Marker-Aware Representation Learning
features.description: Learns biologically meaningful representations per genetic marker, preventing cross-marker interference.

features.label: High-Throughput Similarity Retrieval
features.description: FAISS-based similarity indices enabling low-latency nearest-neighbor search over millions of sequences.

features.label: Scientific Traceability + MLOps
features.description: Full dataset versioning, experiment tracking via DVC and Weights & Biases.

features.label: Risk-Controlled System Engineering
features.description: Designed to mitigate: reproducibility loss, dependency drift, data corruption, model–index mismatch.

algorithms.name: Deterministic Feature Engineering
algorithms.description: Sliding-window k-mer encoding with hash-based conversion, alignment-free.

algorithms.name: Deep Representation Learning (CNN)
algorithms.description: Marker-specific CNN: Embedding → 1D Conv → Global pooling → Projection + normalization.

algorithms.name: Contrastive Learning
algorithms.description: Unsupervised instance-level contrastive learning, no taxonomy label dependency.

algorithms.name: FAISS ANN Retrieval
algorithms.description: Per-marker FAISS indices for low-latency Top-K nearest neighbor search.

algorithms.name: Statistical Novelty Detection
algorithms.description: Distance-based and density-based novelty checks with explicit NOVEL flags.

modules.name: Data Ingestion + Marker Separation
modules.responsibility: Curated reference FASTA ingestion, marker-wise separation, taxonomy annotation

modules.name: Deterministic Preprocessing
modules.responsibility: Sliding-window feature extraction, hash encoding, invalid nucleotide filtering

modules.name: Marker-Specific Embedding Model
modules.responsibility: CNN embedding generation per marker, normalized vector output

modules.name: FAISS Index Layer
modules.responsibility: Per-marker ANN indices, low-latency Top-K retrieval

modules.name: Statistical Novelty Detection Layer
modules.responsibility: Distance/density novelty logic, unsafe assignment rejection

modules.name: MLOps Layer
modules.responsibility: DVC versioning, W&B tracking, cron automation, Docker runtime

results: Alignment-free inference pipeline operational at multi-marker scale (~2.8GB reference databases)
Open-set taxonomy classification with honest NOVEL detection
Explainable, auditable outputs with confidence and similarity evidence
MLOps infrastructure for reproducibility and drift monitoring
